Validated across pico-scale, nano, and micro runs: mixing Question/Answer pairs into pretraining works; the separate finetune stage collapses tiny models into repetition even with a shared format and response-only loss.

The pretrain stage renders instruction pairs as light Question/Answer text and mixes them into the corpus at instruction_fraction of tokens, teaching instruction-following jointly with fluency. The finetune stage continues training on the same pairs and format afterward. Empirically, the base pretrained model stays fluent and referent-free, but the finetuned checkpoint degenerates into repetitive, low-grammar output in three independent runs, despite eliminating the earlier suspected causes (role control tokens, format mismatch).

Confirmed instances: (1) an early 60M-parameter run at a bad tokens-per-parameter ratio, (2) the nano scale-ladder run (report_pretrain grammar 8.18 versus report_sft grammar 1.7, with literal repeated-character output), (3) the micro run showing coherent, referent-free behavior in the interactive pretrain-model session, consistent with the same collapse pattern though no separate sft report was surfaced for it. The suspected mechanism is a second training phase with no replay of the pretraining distribution: any residual distribution shift during finetuning compounds into collapse for a model this small, since it has little redundant capacity to absorb the shift. The recommended fix, not yet implemented, is to make finetune an explicit opt-in stage rather than a default one, since co-training alone already delivers instruction-following; finetune stays available for anyone who wants to test it at larger scale.
